pattern: Brute force over all triples of points using the shoelace cross-product formula to compute each triangle's area and keep the maximum.
hints: The signed area of a triangle from three coordinates comes directly from a determinant of their differences.
With only a few dozen points you can afford to examine every unordered triple.
For each triple compute half the absolute cross product of two edge vectors and track the largest value seen.

edge_inputs.description: five points where an axis-aligned triple is largest
exactly three points form the only triangle
some triples are collinear with zero area
extreme corner points at the value limits
a thin triangle nearly collinear has small but nonzero area

stress.description: random distinct integer points within bounds
stress.note: uniqueness enforced to satisfy the distinct-points constraint
